delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This course will begin with a basic outline of the history of phonetics and will be followed by other aspects such as issues surrounding phonetics, basic features, current research and problem areas for Japanese students.
aims: This course aims to provide students with a basic working knowledge of English phonetics.
goals: To provide a the basic features of English phonetics
To introduce issues related to English pronunciation and phonetics
To provide a broad overview of past and recent advances in English phonology

plan.text: The history and scope of pronunciation instruction
Research on the teaching and acquisition of pronunciation skills
The English sound system: the consonant and vowel systems (segmentals)
The English sound system: connected speech, stress and rhythm (suprasegmentals)
Techniques, tools and technology for pronunciation learning.
The differences between North American English and British English
A comparison of phonetic and phonemic alphabets

textbooks: All materials will be provided by the teacher
prereq: Students should have a reasonable understanding of English in order to comprehend an English lecture and pronunciation issues.
prep: Students will be required to review content of lectures outside of class and to read in preparation for future classes.
teachers: ◎ ショーン バーゴイン
